name: Subtree Min/Max Scans
summary: For each node, scan its whole left subtree for the max and right subtree for the min to check the BST rule, then recurse — O(n^2).
visualizationIntent: init: build and show the input tree from the level-order array; every node idle. validate: a cursor ring lands on the current node (node_id) which turns amber (its frame is on the call stack); before trusting it, the algorithm SCANS its entire left subtree for the maximum and its entire right subtree for the minimum — the cursor sweeps every descendant, which is the O(n^2) cost made visible. If left_max >= value or right_min <= value the node flips red (error) and the answer is false. Otherwise it recurses into both children and a fully-validated node turns blue. done: the boolean answer shows whether the whole tree is a valid BST.

lineExplanations.3: Count how many array slots describe the tree.
lineExplanations.4: Start an empty list of real node objects for the drawing.
lineExplanations.5: Start a lookup from a node's id to its object.
lineExplanations.6: Start a per-node state tracker: unseen, on-stack, done, or invalid.
lineExplanations.7: Loop over every slot to initialize its state.
lineExplanations.9: Point the child-slot walker just past the root.
lineExplanations.10: Loop over every slot to build the node objects.
lineExplanations.11: Only non-null slots become real nodes.
lineExplanations.12: Create this node with its value and two empty child links.
lineExplanations.13: Is there a slot left to be the left child?
lineExplanations.14: Is that candidate slot a real (non-null) node?
lineExplanations.15: Link that slot as this node's left child.
lineExplanations.16: Advance the walker past the left-child slot.
lineExplanations.17: Is there a slot left to be the right child?
lineExplanations.18: Is that candidate slot a real (non-null) node?
lineExplanations.19: Link that slot as this node's right child.
lineExplanations.20: Advance the walker past the right-child slot.
lineExplanations.21: Add the finished node to the tree.
lineExplanations.22: Record the node under its id for O(1) lookup.
lineExplanations.23: The root is slot 0, or nothing when the tree is empty.
lineExplanations.24: Validate the whole tree, starting at the root.
lineExplanations.25: Return whether the tree is a valid BST.
lineExplanations.28: Base case: an absent child is trivially a valid BST.
lineExplanations.29: Return True for a missing node.
lineExplanations.30: Mark this node active — its frame is on the call stack.
lineExplanations.32: Get the id of this node's left child.
lineExplanations.33: Get the id of this node's right child.
lineExplanations.34: Does this node have a left subtree to check?
lineExplanations.35: Scan the whole left subtree for its largest value.
lineExplanations.36: Is that maximum too big (at or above this node)?
lineExplanations.37: Rule broken — mark this node invalid (red).
lineExplanations.38: The tree is not a BST; return False.
lineExplanations.39: Does this node have a right subtree to check?
lineExplanations.40: Scan the whole right subtree for its smallest value.
lineExplanations.41: Is that minimum too small (at or below this node)?
lineExplanations.42: Rule broken — mark this node invalid (red).
lineExplanations.43: The tree is not a BST; return False.
lineExplanations.46: This node is valid only if both subtrees are.
lineExplanations.47: Both subtrees checked — mark this node fully validated.
lineExplanations.51: Base case: an empty subtree has no maximum.
lineExplanations.52: Return a value smaller than any real node.
lineExplanations.53: Start the maximum at this node's own value.
lineExplanations.54: Find the largest value in the left subtree.
lineExplanations.55: Is the left subtree's max bigger than the best so far?
lineExplanations.56: Adopt it as the new maximum.
lineExplanations.57: Find the largest value in the right subtree.
lineExplanations.58: Is the right subtree's max bigger than the best so far?
lineExplanations.59: Adopt it as the new maximum.
lineExplanations.60: Return the largest value found in this subtree.
lineExplanations.63: Base case: an empty subtree has no minimum.
lineExplanations.64: Return a value larger than any real node.
lineExplanations.65: Start the minimum at this node's own value.
lineExplanations.66: Find the smallest value in the left subtree.
lineExplanations.67: Is the left subtree's min smaller than the best so far?
lineExplanations.68: Adopt it as the new minimum.
lineExplanations.69: Find the smallest value in the right subtree.
lineExplanations.70: Is the right subtree's min smaller than the best so far?
lineExplanations.71: Adopt it as the new minimum.
lineExplanations.72: Return the smallest value found in this subtree.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the wrapper class LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def isValidBST(self, values): the entry method; values is the tree as a level-order array.
syntaxExplanations.3: len(values) counts the elements in the input array.
syntaxExplanations.5: by_id = {} creates an empty dictionary (a map from id to node).
syntaxExplanations.6: state_by_id = [] will hold one status number per node id.
syntaxExplanations.7: for i in range(n): loops i from 0 to n-1.
syntaxExplanations.8: append(0) adds a 0 (unseen) entry for this slot.
syntaxExplanations.9: child = 1 sets the next-child pointer to slot 1.
syntaxExplanations.10: for i in range(n): a second pass, this time to build nodes.
syntaxExplanations.11: is not None checks the slot holds a real value.
syntaxExplanations.12: A dict literal { ... } builds this node object.
syntaxExplanations.13: if child < n: is there still a slot available?
syntaxExplanations.14: Check the candidate child slot is not null.
syntaxExplanations.15: node["left"] = child stores the left child's id.
syntaxExplanations.16: child = child + 1 advances to the next slot.
syntaxExplanations.17: if child < n: is there another slot for the right child?
syntaxExplanations.18: Check the candidate right slot is not null.
syntaxExplanations.19: node["right"] = child stores the right child's id.
syntaxExplanations.21: nodes.append(node) adds the built node to the list.
syntaxExplanations.22: by_id[i] = node registers the node under its id.
syntaxExplanations.23: A ? B : C-style conditional: 0 if there is a root, else None.
syntaxExplanations.25: return answer hands back the True/False verdict.
syntaxExplanations.27: def validate(self, node_id, by_id, state_by_id): the recursive checker for one node.
syntaxExplanations.28: if node_id is None: the base case — an absent child.
syntaxExplanations.29: return True: an empty subtree satisfies the rule.
syntaxExplanations.32: by_id[node_id]['left'] is the left child's id (or None).
syntaxExplanations.33: by_id[node_id]['right'] is the right child's id (or None).
syntaxExplanations.34: if left_id is not None: only scan a real left subtree.
syntaxExplanations.35: self.findMax(...) returns the largest value in the left subtree.
syntaxExplanations.36: left_max >= value violates 'left subtree strictly smaller'.
syntaxExplanations.38: return False: the BST rule is broken.
syntaxExplanations.39: if right_id is not None: only scan a real right subtree.
syntaxExplanations.40: self.findMin(...) returns the smallest value in the right subtree.
syntaxExplanations.41: right_min <= value violates 'right subtree strictly larger'.
syntaxExplanations.43: return False: the BST rule is broken.
syntaxExplanations.44: self.validate(left_id, ...) recurses on the left child.
syntaxExplanations.45: self.validate(right_id, ...) recurses on the right child.
syntaxExplanations.46: left_valid and right_valid: both subtrees must be valid.
syntaxExplanations.47: state_by_id[node_id] = 2 marks the node fully validated.
syntaxExplanations.50: def findMax(self, node_id, by_id): returns the max value in a subtree.
syntaxExplanations.51: if node_id is None: an empty subtree.
syntaxExplanations.52: return a very small sentinel so it never wins a max.
syntaxExplanations.53: best starts at this node's own value.
syntaxExplanations.54: recurse to get the left subtree's maximum.
syntaxExplanations.55: if left_max > best: the left side is larger.
syntaxExplanations.56: best = left_max updates the running maximum.
syntaxExplanations.57: recurse to get the right subtree's maximum.
syntaxExplanations.58: if right_max > best: the right side is larger.
syntaxExplanations.59: best = right_max updates the running maximum.
syntaxExplanations.60: return best: the largest value in this subtree.
syntaxExplanations.62: def findMin(self, node_id, by_id): returns the min value in a subtree.
syntaxExplanations.63: if node_id is None: an empty subtree.
syntaxExplanations.64: return a very large sentinel so it never wins a min.
syntaxExplanations.65: best starts at this node's own value.
syntaxExplanations.66: recurse to get the left subtree's minimum.
syntaxExplanations.67: if left_min < best: the left side is smaller.
syntaxExplanations.68: best = left_min updates the running minimum.
syntaxExplanations.69: recurse to get the right subtree's minimum.
syntaxExplanations.70: if right_min < best: the right side is smaller.
syntaxExplanations.71: best = right_min updates the running minimum.
syntaxExplanations.72: return best: the smallest value in this subtree.
